raw_consumer_statement:
American Express has outright refused to acknowledge, address, or engage with the specific claims I submitted in my formal billing dispute regarding a charge for veterinary services that were not properly rendered. I am submitting this complaint to formally reiterate that I am continuing to dispute a charge of {$1500.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, dated XX/XX/XXXX, billed to my American Express account ending in XXXX. 

This charge was previously disputed in XX/XX/XXXX, and again through a formal chargeback request submitted on XX/XX/XXXX, with updated documentation. Amex gave me until XX/XX/XXXX to upload supporting files, which I did on XX/XX/XXXX. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, American Express denied my dispute without meaningfully addressing the newly submitted facts and documents. On XX/XX/XXXX, they responded again ; refusing to treat this as a proper billing dispute and continuing to misclassify it as a tort issue, which it is not. 

I am not seeking personal injury compensation as they stated ; I am seeking a chargeback for services that were either not rendered as agreed or rendered at all. My updated XX/XX/XXXX submission includes : Documentation showing my pet was misdiagnosed Referrals made under false pretenses Improper or inflated billing A lack of proper care or stabilization Amex failed to address any of this in their denials. 

Why I Am Submitting This Complaint Now : As of XX/XX/XXXX, I made a payment of {$5.00} ... the only undisputed amount on my current statement. I did this in good faith, to avoid potential credit harm or late fees. I believe that if I owe something, I will pay it. 

However, I have refused to pay the {$50.00} minimum because that amount includes the disputed XXXX charge and related interest, and I will not continue making payments on a charge that is still being contested and is the source of deep emotional, mental, and financial stress. 

I also wish to clarify that I have already made partial payments toward this disputed charge under duress, out of fear of having my credit harmed. That payment was made under protest, and should not be interpreted as acceptance of the charges legitimacy.

Additional Details : I have filed complaints with : The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ) The New York Attorney General ( NYAG ) Those complaints are active, and I am requesting that CFPB : Reopen this billing dispute and treat it as a proper chargeback under the Fair Credit Billing Act Ensure Amex does not report me late or add further interest while this dispute remains unresolved Ensure my prior and current payments are not used to undermine my right to dispute
